tldr: Michael Valgren took the Marche muri stage solo for EF Education-EasyPost, with Isaac del Toro 2nd and Matteo Jorgenson 3rd same time. Del Toro extended his GC lead by 19 seconds on Pellizzari with bonus-seconds and the late chase.

narrative.headline: Valgren solo on the muri
narrative.body: 186 km, classic Marche steep-walls stage. Valgren bridged off the day's break and held to the line; Del Toro the strongest of the GC contenders chasing back, with Jorgenson on his wheel.

gcImpact.headline: Del Toro extends
gcImpact.body: Del Toro added 19 seconds to Pellizzari on the day; the GC margin grows ahead of the summit finish on Stage 6.

riderPerformances.name: Matteo Jorgenson
riderPerformances.narrative: On Del Toro's wheel through the late chase. Visma's secondary GC card running well after Vingegaard's Paris-Nice win — the Ardennes set-up moving into rhythm.
